channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Kevin O'Leary says he's offering $20B cash for TikTok
item.description: Investor Kevin O’Leary, widely known as a star from “Shark Tank,” said he offered TikTok’s owners $20 billion in cash to buy the platform during a Friday appearance on Fox News’s “America’s Newsroom.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Florida jury says CNN defamed Navy veteran in story about endangered Afghans
item.description: After a Florida jury found that CNN defamed a U.S. Navy veteran who helped rescue endangered Afghans, the network reached a settlement on Friday to avoid a punishing order that it pay punitive damages.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Student admits to releasing toxic gas in dorm, forces evacuations hours after FBI terrorism questioning
item.description: A Utah State University student was arrested after admitting to releasing chemical gas in his dorm hours after being questioned by FBI Terrorism Task Force investigators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Busta Rhymes turns himself in after fight with man in NYC, police say
item.description: Trevor Tanhiem Smith, Jr., 52, also known as Busta Rhymes, is accused of punching a man in the face during a fight last week, police said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Bail denied for American captured in France and accused of assaulting Pennsylvania student
item.description: EAST BERLIN, Pa. (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was ordered held without bail Friday during a court hearing in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: City bus comes within inches of disaster on elevated overpass during rush hour
item.description: Video captured a New York City commuter bus dangling on the edge of an overpass in the Bronx on Friday morning after the driver collided with a stone wall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants one of the agents who covered him after he was shot to lead the Secret Service
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will choose to lead the U.S. Secret Service one of the agents who covered him after a gunmen opened fire at the candidate during a campaign rally in Pennsylvania. Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: PHOTOS: 'Home Alone' house in Illinois sells for $250K over asking price
item.description: A famous house featured in the iconic holiday film "Home Alone" sold this week for nearly $300,000 over its asking price.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration picks 15 more drugs for Medicare negotiation
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) has selected 15 more drugs for Medicare price negotiation, announcing the selection two weeks ahead of schedule.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Fire at one of the world's largest battery plants forces evacuations in California
item.description: Hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate and part of Highway 1 in Northern California was closed after a major fire erupted at one of the world's largest battery storage plants.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State Farm pulls Super Bowl commercial, shifts focus to supporting L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: State Farm has decided to pull its Super Bowl commercial and instead focus on supporting Los Angeles residents impacted by the deadly wildfires. “Our focus is firmly on providing support to the people of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Montana House endorses transgender bathroom ban affecting fellow lawmakers
item.description: Montana’s Republican-controlled House on Wednesday endorsed a ban on transgender people using bathrooms in public buildings that do not align with their sex assigned at birth — a change that would complicate daily life for two fellow lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel and Hamas reach a ceasefire deal
item.description: President Joe Biden says Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire deal to pause the war in Gaza and release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, KRON4

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Actor Bill Hader 'in shock' returning to Palisades Fire neighborhood
item.description: "It's just gone. Everything," "Barry" actor Bill Hader said as he returned to the Pacific Palisades area.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles fire captain loses home to Eaton Fire
item.description: A man tasked with protecting the Los Angeles area from raging wildfires now ranks among those who have lost their homes and everything inside.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WGN 9

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA floats plan to make cigarettes nonaddictive, but its fate rests with Trump
item.description: Federal officials on Wednesday released a far-reaching proposal to make cigarettes less addictive by capping their nicotine content, a goal long sought by antismoking advocates that is unlikely to go into effect anytime soon.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, Fox 5

item.title: US must not become complacent to a growing terrorism threat, a Counterterrorism Center official says
item.description: McLEAN, Va. (AP) — Brett Holmgren got woken up early on New Year's Day by alerts that a driver had plowed into a crowd of revelers in New Orleans. The rampage, which killed 14 people, was the deadliest attack on U.S. soil in years and was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dire fire warning for LA area pushed back as winds ease
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Easing winds delivered a brief but much-needed reprieve to firefighters Tuesday as they battled two massive blazes burning in the Los Angeles area, and the National Weather Service pushed back its unusually dire warning of critical fire weather until early the following day.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dementia cases expected to double by 2060: Study
item.description: Dementia cases in the U.S. are expected to double by 2060 when around 1 million Americans are projected to develop the memory-losing condition every year, according to a new study published Monday in the medical journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
